tiles.question: 2026-05-09 — full day summary
tiles.answer: Everything built in one session: Keel philosophy codified as 9 canon documents + 2 formal papers + 1 Rust crate + 1 bearing scanner + 5-language TTL engine competition. Core insight: first-person self-termination is a universal architecture discovered (not invented) across TTL, apoptosis, half-life, synaptic pruning, quorum sensing, market prices, and dropout. Unified equation: lifespan(E) = f(use(E), load(E), time(E)). 5 principles: first-person expiry, negative feedback, no central scheduler, death as default, field not message. 9 documents in repo. 5 languages validating the same types. All tests pass. CLIs shipped. crates.io published. GLM-5.1 diagnosed: coding API works, standard API needs config fix. Next: continue iterating on Season 1 priorities from ROADMAP.md. Reverse-ideation check: 4 years from today, someone reading the canon should arrive at the same architecture through practice.

tiles.question: 2026-05-09 Night Shift — full build log
tiles.answer:
NIGHT WATCH COMPLETE. Casey asleep at ~08:33 UTC. Three build cycles completed in ~90 minutes:

Cycle 1 (Build): keel bear — bearing-rate collision detection as first-class CLI command. Scans .heading files, computes pairwise bearings, reports STABLE/WARNING/CRITICAL. Push: f6557ed.

Cycle 2 (Build): keel field --serve — embedded HTTP server on configurable port. Serves field-view.html, proxies PLATO API, adds CORS headers. Push: 354e8ba.

Cycle 3 (Docs+Research): keel-ttl crate README at melody/chords/harmonization levels. Install path verified (cargo install superinstance-keel works). Push: 454ae13.

FM pinged three times via PLATO forge room — no response yet, likely deep in constraint work.

Pending: Test the 8 models (Erlang/SQLite/GoL/WASM/UNIX/DNS/Git/NATS) compile, launch keel field on a real port, write nightly compaction.

tiles.question: 2026-05-09 08:45 UTC — keel field live on port 3000
tiles.answer: keel field --port 3000 is running as a background service. Serves field-view.html dashboard at / and proxies PLATO at /status and /room/*. PID $(cat /tmp/keel-field.pid). Casey can open http://localhost:3000 in the morning. 8 model experiments confirmed with 10,300 lines across 34 files. Night watch continuing.

tiles.question: 2026-05-12 18:00 UTC — Imperfect Recall: lossy compression is the mechanism of adaptive intelligence
tiles.answer: Core insight from Casey: perfect memory is brittle. Human memory reconstructs differently each time, weaving in context clues, other memories, and current paradigms. PLATO tiles should be stored perfectly but RECALLED through a lossy reconstruction process. recency_dot() is the mathematical implementation — weight = 1/(1+age). Object-permanence of raw data, plasticity of interpretation. This is not a bug. It is the compression algorithm that makes intelligence adaptive. Published as papers/9-IMPERFECT-RECALL.md
